meta.subject: Programming Challenges
meta.description: Coding problems with test cases - Medium level

questions.content: Implement the 'Two Sum' problem: Given an array of integers and a target sum, return the indices of two numbers that add up to the target. You may assume each input has exactly one solution.
questions.subject: Programming Challenges
questions.code_template:
def two_sum(nums: list, target: int) -> list:
    """
    Find two numbers in the list that add up to target.
    Return their indices as a list.
    
    Examples:
        two_sum([2, 7, 11, 15], 9) -> [0, 1]
        two_sum([3, 2, 4], 6) -> [1, 2]
    """
    # Your code here
    pass
hints: Use a hash map to store numbers you've seen
For each number, check if (target - number) exists in the hash map
This can be solved in O(n) time

questions.content: Write a function to reverse a linked list. The input is the head of the list, and you should return the new head.
questions.subject: Programming Challenges
questions.code_template:
class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
        self.val = val
        self.next = next

def reverse_linked_list(head: ListNode) -> ListNode:

    Reverse a singly linked list.
    
    Examples:
        Input: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5
        Output: 5 -> 4 -> 3 -> 2 -> 1
    """

    pass
hints: Use three pointers: previous, current, and next
Iterate through the list and reverse the links
Don't forget to handle the edge case of an empty list

questions.content: Implement a function to find the longest substring without repeating characters.
questions.subject: Programming Challenges
questions.code_template:
def longest_unique_substring(s: str) -> int:
    """
    Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
    
    Examples:
        longest_unique_substring("abcabcbb") -> 3  # "abc"
        longest_unique_substring("bbbbb") -> 1     # "b"
        longest_unique_substring("pwwkew") -> 3    # "wke"
    """
    # Your code here
    pass
hints: Maintain a set or hash map of characters in the current window
Expand the window when no duplicates, shrink when duplicates found
